title: Add a Cloudflare binding to a Worker
description: Wires a binding (KV, R2, D1, Durable Object, Queue, Workflow, or Service/RPC) into a Worker's wrangler.jsonc, stubs the required code (DO/Workflow classes, queue consumer) and migrations, emits a matching test, and refreshes `wrangler types`.

project.description: The target Worker project to add the binding to.
project.x-prompt: Which project would you like to add the binding to?

type.description: The kind of binding to add.
type.x-prompt: What type of binding do you want to add? (kv, r2, d1, do, queue, workflow, service)

binding.description: The Env key name (SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE) the Worker uses to access the binding, e.g. MY_KV.
binding.x-prompt: What name should the binding have in `env`? (SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE, e.g. MY_KV)

name.description: Required for `do`/`workflow` (the exported class name, PascalCase), `queue` (the queue name), and `service` (the target service name). Not used for `kv`/`r2`/`d1`.
name.x-prompt: What is the class name (do/workflow), queue name (queue), or service name (service)?

id.description: Existing KV namespace id or D1 database id. Skipped if `--create` is set (the provisioned id is captured automatically).

databaseName.description: D1 database name. Required when `--type=d1` and `--create` is not set.

bucketName.description: R2 bucket name. Required when `--type=r2` and `--create` is not set.

create.description: Provision the remote resource via the Wrangler CLI (KV/R2/D1/Queue only). Fills the id/name automatically. On provision failure, leaves a placeholder in the config for manual fill-in.

skipTests.description: Skip generating a Vitest spec for the binding stub.

skipTypegen.description: Skip auto-running `wrangler types` after writing the binding. The generated `Env` interface will not be refreshed.
